rulings.Case Title: UNIONN LLC A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY VS. ROBERT H ELMAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Robert Elman'S Motion For Summary Judgment Or, Alternatively, Summary Adjudication Of Plaintiff Unionn Llcs Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 16, 2019 line 1. Defendant Robert Elman'S Motion For Summary Judgment Or, Alternatively, Summary Adjudication Of Plaintiff Unionn Llcs Complaint is DENIED. Defendant failed to show that there are no triable issues of material fact. The Court finds triable issues of material facts as to each of the three causes of action, including the claim for punitive damages, as following: " Whether Defendant was not aware that windows, doors, faucets, sinks, and bathtubs were not operating properly, despite Ms. Batchelder's prior occupancy in the Subject Property. (Separate Statement in Support of Opposition 7, 28, 60, 100, 105,110, 120; Elman Decl., 5,7; Batchelder Decl. 3, 15.) " Whether the bus stop in front of the Subject Property was within Ms. Mohager's diligent attention. (Separate Statement in Support of Opposition 10, 21, 33, 42, 53, 72, 83, 93, 115, 125; McFarland Decl., Ex. B [Mohager Depo.] pp. 250: 11-25 to 251: 1-23; McFarland Decl., Ex. C [Hatvany Depo.] pp. 84-94:1; McFarland Decl., Ex. A. [Batchelder Depo.] pp. 30: 14-25.) " Whether Ms. Mohager was prevented from conducting a proper inspection of the Subject Property during her visits. (Separate Statement in Support of Opposition 9, 20, 32, 41, 52, 71, 82 89, 92, 97, 114, 124; FAC at 10, 17; Andreoli Decl., Ex. 19 at Nos. 7, 28-33; McFarland Decl., Ex. B [Mohager Depo.] pp. 76: 18-25 to 77: 1-20; 102: 1-12.) " Whether Defendant did not know if the sunroom was permitted or not at the time of the sale. (Separate Statement in Support of Opposition 5, 60, 100, 120; Batchelder Decl. 14; Ex. 5.) " Whether the disclosures were made pursuant to the best knowledge of Defendant. (Separate Statement in Support of Opposition 8, 19, 31, 51, 63, 81, 101, 106, 111, 123; Batchelder Decl. 8; McFarland Decl., Ex. C [Hatvany Depo.] pp. 116:8-25 to 117:8-13.) D has failed to shift burden as to whether the AVID form/MUNI schedules disclose the existence of the bus stop in front of the Subject Property. (Separate Statement in Support of Opposition 3, 16, 26, 38, 48, 58, 68, 78, 88, 118; Batchelder Decl., Ex. 4; Andreoli Decl., Ex. 28 (MUNI schedule).) Objection to Batchelder Decl. 15, 6:27-7:5 OVERRULED. Objection to Elman Decl. 7, 3:3-5 OVERRULED. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: FAHIMA ZAHIR VS. 18 LANSING STREET OWNERS'S ASSOCIATION ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 16, 2019 line 2. DEFENDANT 18 LANSING STREET OWNERS'S ASSOCIATION MOTION TO STRIKE 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT is DENIED. TAC sets forth the elements for punitive damages under Civil Code § 3294 and provides sufficient allegations that may rise to the level of malice, oppression or fraud. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: FAHIMA ZAHIR VS. 18 LANSING STREET OWNERS'S ASSOCIATION ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 16, 2019 line 2. DEFENDANT 18 LANSING STREET OWNERS'S ASSOCIATION DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT is OVERRRULED, 10 days to answer. Plaintiff complied with Court's previous ruling that "if Plaintiff cannot in good faith allege that Association is the owner Plaintiff shall name other members as actual defendants, not nominal defendants." = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: FAHIMA ZAHIR VS. 18 LANSING STREET OWNERS'S ASSOCIATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Disqualification Of Plaintiffs Counsel And Protective Order Regarding Unauthorized Publication Of Attorney Client Communications
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 16, 2019 line 2. DEFENDANT 18 LANSING STREET OWNERS'S ASSOCIATION Motion For Disqualification Of Plaintiffs Counsel And Protective Order Regarding Unauthorized Publication Of Attorney Client Communications is DENIED. Defendants have not demonstrated irreversible or unmitigable damage consistent with the Rico decision. If there are other specific legal grounds mandating disqualification on facts alleged, such grounds have not been provided in this motion. The Protective Order is determined to be moot as to mediation notes as the contents thereof have been disclosed. An appropriate objection may be made at the time any party seeks to introduce notes. As to the opinion letters, the Court finds that any privilege has been waived. Objections to Sidebotham's Declarations 2,5,7 OVERRULED. Objection to Zahir's Declaration 3 OVERRULED. Request to Strike all of Zahir's exhibits DENIED. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: LANCE EVANDER ET AL VS. VERITAS INVESTMENTS, INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 16, 2019 line 3. DEFENDANT VERITAS INVESTMENTS, INC., GREENTREE PROPERTY MANAGEMENT, INC., YAT AU, 240 SAINT JOSEPHS, LLC, 240 SAINT JOSEPHS A2, LP, 318 TURK I7, LP, 1440 SUTTER STREET, LLC, 57 TAYLOR I7, LP, 655 POWELL I5 LLC, 2730 SACRAMENTO I4, LLC, 3659 20TH STREET, LLC, 916 PACIFIC I5, LLC, 1075 O'FARRELL STREET LLC, 920 LEAVENWORTH I5 LLC, 990 FULTON I2 LLC, 337 10TH I5 LLC, 845 CALIFORNIA STREET LLC, 709 GEARY STREET LLC, 621 STOCKTON PROPERTY, LLC, 336-350 JUDAH I3, LP, 698 BUSH C1, LP, 915 PIERCE STREET, LLC, 1819 GOLDEN GATE I1 LLC, 270 TURK, LLC, 610-660 CLIPPER I1, LLC, 721 GEARY I5, LLC, 720 BAKER I1, LLC, SF 685 GEARY STREET, LLC, 3201 23RD I7 LP, 520 BUCHANAN STREET LLC, 1126 BUSH STREET LLC, 20 SAN ANTONIO I6 LP, I6-50 LANGUNA I2 LLC, 1064 DOLORES I1 LLC,, 1260 BROADWAY STREET, LLC, 1025 STEINER I2 LLC, 655 STOCKTON STREET, LLC, 665 PINE I7, LP, SF 3809 20TH STREET, LLC, 1035 SUTTER I3, LP, 516 ELLIS I7, LP, 990 GEARY I3, LP, VIC C16, LLC, 1 SAN ANTONIO I5, LLC DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT SUSTAINED with leave to amend (including the amendments requested by the Motion for Leave to Amend) as follows: (1) Plaintiffs to allege each fact in support of each cause of action against each defendant. Causes of action are to be arranged by plaintiff under separate counts specifying a plaintiff and each defendant against whom the cause of action is alleged; Plaintiff may additionally arrange the plaintiffs by building; (2) Plaintiff to allege facts support its theory of a "business plan to force out long-term tenants in rent-controlled apartment; this allegations to be under a separate heading within General Allegations. The Court will not rule on the misjoinder argument at this time. Complaint as currently alleged appears to be improper. However, such determination cannot be made without in light of the fact that there is no viable cause of action alleged by any of the plaintiffs and it is not clear what business plan, i.e. the same transaction or occurrence is. Plaintiffs shall have 60 days to file an amended complaint mindful of the Court's inclinations regarding misjoinder and shall only file an amended complaint in good faith. = (501/CFH)
